data.narrative:
AAVantgarde Bio is a Series B–stage biotech company founded by Alberto Auricchio and Natalia Misciattelli. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, with four direct Series B comparables: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $141M in total raised as a base proxy, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B–to–next-stage advancement, yielding $268M. Peer-median triangulation across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range from $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed—a standard constraint for private biotech companies.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $228M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $784M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding AAVantgarde's clinical progress, IP position, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Stage-progression yields a conservative floor; peer-median suggests meaningfully higher value but depends critically on which peer cohort proves most comparable. Further narrowing would require transparency on development stage, cash runway, and any differentiated therapeutic or platform claims.
